meta.grounding: Portable seamanship — valid under any code.

questions.stem: At sea, what does the regulation say about discharging rubbish and hydrocarbons?

choices.text: It is prohibited
It is free beyond 3 miles
It is allowed at night

provenance.ref: Code de l'environnement, art. L.218-11 et s. / MARPOL annexes I et V
provenance.source: Code de l'environnement — pollution par les rejets des navires (art. L.218-11 et s.) / convention MARPOL
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: Discharging rubbish and hydrocarbons into the sea is prohibited; waste must be brought back ashore.

questions.stem: What should the boater do with waste produced aboard?

choices.text: Bring it ashore to harbour facilities
Throw it overboard offshore
Burn it aboard

provenance.ref: MARPOL annexe V / Code de l'environnement — déchets rapportés à terre
provenance.source: Code de l'environnement — pollution par les rejets des navires (art. L.218-11 et s.) / convention MARPOL
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: Keep it aboard and dispose of it ashore in the harbour's collection facilities.

questions.stem: In the Mediterranean, anchoring on Posidonia seagrass beds is:

choices.text: Regulated, even prohibited (protected beds)
Free and harmless
Recommended because the anchor holds well there

provenance.ref: PREMAR Méditerranée, arrêté n° 123/2019, art. 6 — mouillage / posidonie
provenance.source: Arrêtés des préfets maritimes (bande littorale des 300 m ; mouillage et protection de la posidonie)
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: Regulated, and prohibited in many areas: the anchor tears up these protected beds, vital to marine life; use the designated anchoring zones or buoys.

questions.stem: The Beaufort scale, measuring wind force, is graduated from 0 to what value?

provenance.source: Météo-France / OMM — échelle de Beaufort (force 0 à 12)
provenance.licence: Échelle de Beaufort (OMM) — barème international du domaine public.

questions.explanation: The Beaufort scale runs from force 0 (calm) to force 12 (hurricane).

questions.stem: On a nautical chart, relative to what level are the depths (soundings) given?

choices.text: Chart datum (lowest tides)
Mean sea level
High-water level

provenance.ref: SHOM — zéro hydrographique (sondes des cartes marines)
provenance.source: SHOM — Service hydrographique et océanographique de la marine (cartes et marées : zéro hydrographique, marnage, coefficient, étale)
provenance.licence: SHOM — données et références hydrographiques officielles (réutilisation sous Licence Ouverte / Etalab).

questions.explanation: Soundings are referred to chart datum, close to the level of the lowest tides: actual depth is usually greater.

questions.stem: What is the “tidal range” (marnage)?

choices.text: The height difference between high and low water
The speed of the tidal stream
The duration of a tide

provenance.ref: SHOM — marnage (pleine mer / basse mer consécutives)
provenance.source: SHOM — Service hydrographique et océanographique de la marine (cartes et marées : zéro hydrographique, marnage, coefficient, étale)
provenance.licence: SHOM — données et références hydrographiques officielles (réutilisation sous Licence Ouverte / Etalab).

questions.explanation: The tidal range is the height difference between a high water and the following (or preceding) low water.

questions.stem: Between what values does the tidal coefficient vary?

choices.text: Between 20 and 120
Between 0 and 100
Between 1 and 12

provenance.source: SHOM — Service hydrographique et océanographique de la marine (cartes et marées : zéro hydrographique, marnage, coefficient, étale)
provenance.licence: SHOM — données et références hydrographiques officielles (réutilisation sous Licence Ouverte / Etalab).

questions.explanation: The tidal coefficient ranges from 20 (very weak neap) to 120 (very strong spring); 95 marks the threshold of large tides.

questions.stem: What is the “rule of twelfths” used for?

choices.text: To estimate the water height at a given hour of the tide
To calculate the wind force
To measure drift from the current

provenance.ref: Marées — règle des douzièmes (méthode standard, SHOM)
provenance.source: SHOM — Service hydrographique et océanographique de la marine (cartes et marées : zéro hydrographique, marnage, coefficient, étale)
provenance.licence: SHOM — données et références hydrographiques officielles (réutilisation sous Licence Ouverte / Etalab).

questions.explanation: It estimates the height of water at a given time between low and high water (1/12, 2/12, 3/12, 3/12, 2/12, 1/12 per hour).

questions.stem: What is the tidal “slack” (étale)?

choices.text: When the water level stops changing (slack of the level)
When the current is strongest
The difference between high and low water

provenance.source: SHOM — Service hydrographique et océanographique de la marine (cartes et marées : zéro hydrographique, marnage, coefficient, étale)
provenance.licence: SHOM — données et références hydrographiques officielles (réutilisation sous Licence Ouverte / Etalab).

questions.explanation: The brief moment, at high or low water, when the level stops changing (slack of the level). The current's turn (slack of stream) is a related but distinct event that need not coincide.

questions.stem: Up to what distance from a shelter does the côtière option of the permis plaisance authorise navigation?

choices.text: 6 nautical miles
2 nautical miles
With no distance limit

provenance.ref: Décret n° 2007-1167 du 2 août 2007, art. 2 — portée de l'option côtière (6 milles)
provenance.source: Décret n° 2007-1167 du 2 août 2007 relatif au permis de conduire des bateaux de plaisance à moteur
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: The côtière option allows navigation up to 6 nautical miles from a shelter, day and night, regardless of engine power.

questions.stem: Which navigation programme does design category “C” of a pleasure boat correspond to?

choices.text: Inshore (force 6, 2 m waves)
Unrestricted ocean
Sheltered waters only

provenance.ref: Directive 2013/53/UE, annexe I, partie A — catégories de conception (C : force 6, vagues 2 m)
provenance.source: Directive 2013/53/UE relative aux bateaux de plaisance — catégories de conception (annexe I, partie A)
provenance.licence: © Union européenne, eur-lex.europa.eu — réutilisation autorisée (décision 2011/833/UE).

questions.explanation: Category C = “inshore”: designed for winds up to force 6 and waves up to 2 m. (A = ocean, B = offshore, D = sheltered waters.)

questions.stem: What should the boater do with waste water (black water) on inland waters?

choices.text: Hold it in a tank and empty it ashore
Discharge it discreetly at night
Discharge it far from the banks

provenance.ref: Code des transports, art. R.4241-63 / CDNI (décret 2010-197) — eaux usées (cuve, dépôt à terre)
provenance.source: Code des transports — navigation intérieure (titre de navigation, alcoolémie, règlements particuliers de police)
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: Keep it in a holding tank aboard, then empty it at shore collection facilities; discharging it into the waterway is prohibited.

questions.stem: On inland waters, discharging hydrocarbons, waste water or rubbish into the waterway is:

choices.text: Prohibited
Allowed in small amounts
Free outside harbours

provenance.ref: RGP (Code des transports, art. R.4241-62) — rejet d'hydrocarbures interdit
provenance.source: Règlement général de police de la navigation intérieure (RGP/RGPNI) — Code des transports, art. R. 4241-1 et s.
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: Prohibited: such discharges pollute the waterway and must be collected and disposed of in the proper facilities.

questions.stem: To limit bank erosion and disturbance to wildlife, the boater should above all:

choices.text: Reduce speed and wash near the banks
Run at full speed in the centre
Moor in the reed beds

provenance.ref: RGP, art. A.4241-53-21 — réduire vitesse et batillage près des berges
provenance.source: Règlement général de police de la navigation intérieure (RGP/RGPNI) — Code des transports, art. R. 4241-1 et s.
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: Reduce speed and wash when approaching banks, reed beds and sensitive natural areas.

questions.stem: When refuelling, what precaution prevents water pollution?

choices.text: Avoid any overflow (do not fill to the brim)
Fill as fast as possible
Rinse the tank with canal water

provenance.ref: RGP (Code des transports, art. R.4241-62) — prévention de la pollution
provenance.source: Règlement général de police de la navigation intérieure (RGP/RGPNI) — Code des transports, art. R. 4241-1 et s.
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: Refuel unhurriedly and do not fill to the brim (use a funnel, watch the level) to avoid any fuel spilling into the water.

questions.stem: A vessel's identification mark (registration) must be:

choices.text: Displayed visibly and legibly on the hull
Kept only in the boat's papers
Optional for pleasure craft

provenance.ref: RGP, art. A.4241-47-1 — marque d'identification du bateau
provenance.source: Règlement général de police de la navigation intérieure (RGP/RGPNI) — Code des transports, art. R. 4241-1 et s.
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: It must be displayed visibly and legibly on the hull, so the vessel can be identified.

questions.stem: Before getting under way, what must the skipper check (duty of care)?

choices.text: The boat's condition, the safety gear and the weather
Only the fuel level
Nothing — checks are the lock's job

provenance.ref: RGP, art. R.4241-9/-10 — devoir général de prudence du conducteur
provenance.source: Règlement général de police de la navigation intérieure (RGP/RGPNI) — Code des transports, art. R. 4241-1 et s.
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: Ensure the boat and engine are sound, the safety equipment is aboard, and the conditions (water level, weather) are suitable.

questions.stem: According to ONI art. 142, which ordinance do the definitions of "risk analysis", "safety report" and "duty of diligence" refer to?

choices.text: To the ordinance of 14 March 1994 on the construction of vessels (OCEB).
To the Inland Navigation Ordinance (ONI) itself.
To the Navigation Regulations for Lake Geneva (RNL).

provenance.source: Ordonnance sur la navigation intérieure (ONI), RS 747.201.1
provenance.licence: Public domain — Swiss federal law (freely reusable).

questions.explanation: ONI art. 142 refers, for these concepts, to the articles of the OCEB (ordinance of 14 March 1994 on the construction of vessels).

questions.stem: Among these statements about the definitions in ONI art. 142, which one is FALSE?

choices.text: The "duty of diligence" is defined in ONI art. 142 without reference to another ordinance.
The "safety report" is a report within the meaning of art. 2, let. e, and art. 17, para. 3, OCEB.
The "expert examination report" is a report within the meaning of art. 2, let. f, OCEB.

provenance.source: Ordonnance sur la navigation intérieure (ONI), RS 747.201.1
provenance.licence: Public domain — Swiss federal law (freely reusable).

questions.explanation: According to ONI art. 142, let. e, the "duty of diligence" is defined by reference to art. 5 OCEB, and not without reference.

questions.stem: According to ONI art. 142, how are the "experts" defined within the meaning of the special provisions of this section?

choices.text: As the persons referred to in art. 5a, para. 1, let. a, OCEB.
As the officers of the competent authority of the place of mooring.
As the members of a recognized classification society.

provenance.source: Ordonnance sur la navigation intérieure (ONI), RS 747.201.1
provenance.licence: Public domain — Swiss federal law (freely reusable).

questions.explanation: According to ONI art. 142, let. c, the "experts" are the persons referred to in art. 5a, para. 1, let. a, OCEB.

questions.stem: What is a “pushed convoy” according to the ONI?

choices.text: An assembly of non-propelled vessels joined together into a rigid unit, pushed by at least one motorised vessel
An assembly of non-propelled vessels towed by at least one motorised vessel
Any group of pleasure craft navigating together

provenance.source: Ordonnance sur la navigation intérieure (ONI), RS 747.201.1
provenance.licence: Public domain — Swiss federal law (freely reusable).

questions.explanation: ONI art. 2: the pushed convoy is a rigid unit that is pushed; if towed = towed convoy.

questions.stem: Within the meaning of the ONI, what does the term "motorized vessel" designate?

choices.text: A vessel with mechanical propulsion
A non-propelled vessel towed by another vessel
A floating device such as a dredger or a crane

provenance.source: Ordonnance sur la navigation intérieure (ONI), RS 747.201.1
provenance.licence: Public domain — Swiss federal law (freely reusable).

questions.explanation: ONI art. 2: a "motorized vessel" is a vessel with mechanical propulsion.

questions.stem: According to RNL art. 71, does navigation in restricted visibility also concern situations that are not explicitly listed?

choices.text: Yes, it also covers any other conditions limiting visibility in the same manner.
No, only the conditions specifically cited (mist, fog, etc.) are covered.
Yes, but only conditions caused by smoke from a fire.

provenance.source: Règlement de la navigation sur le Léman (RNL), RS 747.221.1
provenance.licence: Public domain — Franco-Swiss convention / Swiss federal law.

questions.explanation: RNL art. 71 extends the concept to any other conditions limiting visibility in the same manner.

questions.stem: According to RNL art. 71, what is considered navigation in restricted visibility?

choices.text: Any navigation, by day as by night, in mist, fog, drizzle, snow or heavy squalls with rain.
Only navigation at night in thick fog.
Only navigation by day when the rain is accompanied by strong wind.

provenance.source: Règlement de la navigation sur le Léman (RNL), RS 747.221.1
provenance.licence: Public domain — Franco-Swiss convention / Swiss federal law.

questions.explanation: RNL art. 71 defines restricted visibility as any navigation, by day as by night, in mist, fog, drizzle, snow or heavy squalls with rain.

questions.stem: Among these statements about restricted visibility within the meaning of RNL art. 71, which one is FALSE?

choices.text: Navigation in restricted visibility applies only to navigation at night.
Navigation in snowy weather is considered navigation in restricted visibility.
Navigation in drizzly weather is considered navigation in restricted visibility.

provenance.source: Règlement de la navigation sur le Léman (RNL), RS 747.221.1
provenance.licence: Public domain — Franco-Swiss convention / Swiss federal law.

questions.explanation: RNL art. 71 states that restricted visibility applies both by day and by night, and not only at night.

questions.stem: What does the text confirm about the bowline when it is not under tension?

choices.text: It is easy to undo, even when wet and after having been subjected to a very strong pull or to tightening caused by drying out.
It becomes impossible to undo as soon as it gets wet.
It can only be adjusted by applying strong tension to the standing part.

provenance.source: Wikipédia — nœuds marins (matelotage)
provenance.licence: CC BY-SA 4.0 — Wikipédia, attribution required.

questions.explanation: According to Matelotage — Nœud de chaise : Intérêts, when not under tension it is easy to undo even when wet and after a strong pull.

questions.stem: According to the text, how does the bowline react when tension increases?

choices.text: The stronger the tension, the more it locks, while still untying just as easily.
The stronger the tension, the more it tends to slip and come undone.
Tension has no effect on its locking, which depends on an intermediate mechanical system.

provenance.source: Wikipédia — nœuds marins (matelotage)
provenance.licence: CC BY-SA 4.0 — Wikipédia, attribution required.

questions.explanation: According to Matelotage — Nœud de chaise : Intérêts, the stronger the tension, the more the knot locks and the more easily it unties.

questions.stem: According to the text, the bowline is also called:

choices.text: Bowline knot (nœud de bouline).
Clove hitch.
Hangman's knot.

provenance.source: Wikipédia — nœuds marins (matelotage)
provenance.licence: CC BY-SA 4.0 — Wikipédia, attribution required.

questions.explanation: According to Matelotage — Nœud de chaise : Présentation, the bowline is also called the nœud de bouline.

questions.stem: Why is the bowline described as the king of knots, according to the text?

choices.text: It is a reliable loop knot that does not slip under tension and unties easily even after strong tension.
It is the only knot that is impossible to untie once tightened.
It can only be made with a specialized tool and with two hands.

provenance.ref: Matelotage — Nœud de chaise : Roi des nœuds
provenance.source: Wikipédia — nœuds marins (matelotage)
provenance.licence: CC BY-SA 4.0 — Wikipédia, attribution required.

questions.explanation: According to Matelotage — Nœud de chaise : Roi des nœuds, its quality as a reliable loop knot that does not slip and unties easily justifies it.

questions.stem: Why is the bowline described as the “king of knots”?

choices.text: It is a reliable loop knot that does not slip under tension and unties easily
It tightens more and more under tension until it becomes impossible to untie
It is used only to join two ropes of the same diameter

provenance.ref: Matelotage — Nœud de chaise : Roi des nœuds
provenance.source: Wikipédia — nœuds marins (matelotage)
provenance.licence: CC BY-SA 4.0 — Wikipédia, attribution required.

questions.explanation: The bowline is a reliable loop that does not slip and unties easily.

questions.stem: Regarding the methods for making the bowline, which statement(s) does the text confirm?

choices.text: There are several methods for making it, up to 18 including the variants.
Only two classic methods are recognized for teaching, knitted or prepared.
A single unique method of making it is accepted throughout the world.

provenance.ref: Matelotage — Nœud de chaise : Roi des nœuds
provenance.source: Wikipédia — nœuds marins (matelotage)
provenance.licence: CC BY-SA 4.0 — Wikipédia, attribution required.

questions.explanation: According to Matelotage — Nœud de chaise : Roi des nœuds, there are up to 18 methods including variants but two classic teaching methods.

questions.stem: According to the text, what does the hangman's knot illustrate regarding bight knots?

choices.text: It can be easily undone if the loop is free, but tightens around the restraint under the weight of the hanged person.
It can never be undone, whether the loop is free or restrained.
It loosens automatically as soon as weight is applied to the loop.

provenance.source: Wikipédia — nœuds marins (matelotage)
provenance.licence: CC BY-SA 4.0 — Wikipédia, attribution required.

questions.explanation: According to Matelotage — Nœud (lien) : Ganses, the hangman's knot comes undone if the loop is free but tightens under the weight of the hanged person.

questions.stem: According to the text, what is a bight (ganse)?

choices.text: A curved portion of rope, with no crossing or overlapping.
A tight crossing of two rope ends.
A rope braided from three metal strands.

provenance.source: Wikipédia — nœuds marins (matelotage)
provenance.licence: CC BY-SA 4.0 — Wikipédia, attribution required.

questions.explanation: According to Matelotage — Nœud (lien) : Ganses, a bight is a curved portion of rope with no crossing or overlapping.

questions.stem: According to the text, what is the strength of a rope fitted with a bowline compared to a clove hitch?

choices.text: The bowline retains an exceptional strength of 89%, whereas the clove hitch halves it.
The clove hitch retains 89% of the strength, against half for the bowline.
Both knots reduce the rope's strength by exactly half.

provenance.source: Wikipédia — nœuds marins (matelotage)
provenance.licence: CC BY-SA 4.0 — Wikipédia, attribution required.

questions.explanation: According to Matelotage — Nœud (lien) : Propriétés, the clove hitch reduces strength by half whereas the bowline reaches 89%.

questions.stem: According to the text, which criterion makes it possible to evaluate and choose the most suitable knot?

choices.text: The strength of the knot, in particular whether or not it weakens the rope in case of violent jerks.
The color of the rope used to make it.
The purchase price of the cord.

provenance.source: Wikipédia — nœuds marins (matelotage)
provenance.licence: CC BY-SA 4.0 — Wikipédia, attribution required.

questions.explanation: According to Matelotage — Nœud (lien) : Propriétés, the strength of the knot is paramount, especially in case of violent jerks.

questions.stem: According to the text, which statement about the use of knots is correct?

choices.text: The art of knots mainly developed in sailing vessels and then in sport or leisure boating.
Knots have never been used outside the maritime domain.
Knowledge of knots has become completely useless since the appearance of specialized equipment.

provenance.source: Wikipédia — nœuds marins (matelotage)
provenance.licence: CC BY-SA 4.0 — Wikipédia, attribution required.

questions.explanation: According to Matelotage — Nœud (lien) : Présentation, knots developed in sailing vessels and then in boating.

questions.stem: According to the text, what is a knot?

choices.text: The intertwining or interlacing of one or more ropes or other flexible, thread-like objects.
Exclusively the interlacing of two rigid metal cables.
An assembly of planks intended to reinforce a hull.

provenance.source: Wikipédia — nœuds marins (matelotage)
provenance.licence: CC BY-SA 4.0 — Wikipédia, attribution required.

questions.explanation: According to Matelotage — Nœud (lien) : Présentation, a knot is the intertwining or interlacing of flexible, thread-like objects.

questions.stem: What does this signal mean?

choices.text: Boat landing area
The width of the passage is limited
Strong wind warning

provenance.source: Ordonnance sur la navigation intérieure (ONI), RS 747.201.1
provenance.licence: Public domain — Swiss federal law (freely reusable).

questions.explanation: ONI Annexe 4 – fig. 61 — « Strong wind warning ».

questions.stem: What does this signal mean?

choices.text: Storm warning
Dam
Strong wind warning

provenance.source: Ordonnance sur la navigation intérieure (ONI), RS 747.201.1
provenance.licence: Public domain — Swiss federal law (freely reusable).
